answer:
We are evaluating  
\[
\lim_{x \to 1^{-}} \prod_{n=0}^{\infty} \left( \frac{1+x^{n+1}}{1+x^n} \right)^{x^n}.
\]  
Notice that the product telescopes:  
\[
\prod_{n=0}^{N} \frac{1+x^{n+1}}{1+x^n} = \frac{1+x^{N+1}}{1+x^0} = \frac{1+x^{N+1}}{2}.
\]  
Thus the product becomes  
\[
\lim_{N \to \infty} \left( \frac{1+x^{N+1}}{2} \right)^{\sum_{n=0}^N x^n}.
\]  
As \(x \to 1^{-}\), \(x^{N+1} \to 0\) for each finite \(N\), but the exponent \(\sum_{n=0}^N x^n \
